---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*.



```{r}
# clearing the workspace
rm(list=ls(all=TRUE))
```

```{r}

view_flag<-TRUE
start_date <- "2014-08-01"
end_date <- "2019-12-01"

```

```{r}
# Loading the libraries that may prove useful
library("readxl")
library("stringi")
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(skimr)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(sqldf)
library(kableExtra)
library(plm)
library(lmtest)


# library(xlsx)
library(reshape2)
library(tibble)
library(panelr)
library(multiway)
library(stringr)
library(zoo)
library(arsenal)
library("data.table")
library(readr)
library(sqldf)
library(stringr)
library(fuzzyjoin)
library(lubridate)
library(MatchIt)
library(janitor)
library(forcats)
library(officer)
library(magrittr)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12))
```

```{r setup}
# This sets the working directory for the subsequent chunks but not the current chunk. Its a bit weird
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = normalizePath("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/Phd/ABFRL/R_directory"))


```


```{r}

store_list <- read_excel("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_Store Master - PT.xlsx")

#clean names
store_list <- store_list %>%
  clean_names()


n_distinct(store_list$site_code)


#summary df of site_code per city
city_summary <- store_list %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  summarise(num_stores = n_distinct(site_code)) %>%
  arrange(desc(num_stores))




p_hist <- ggplot(city_summary, aes(x = num_stores)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of Number of Stores per City",
    x = "Number of Stores",
    y = "Count of Cities"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()




hist_data2 <- ggplot_build(p_hist)$data[[1]]


p_hist <- p_hist+
  geom_text(
    data = hist_data2,
    aes(x = x, y = count, label = count),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 3
  ) 


print(p_hist)

out_folder <- "C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/Phd/ABFRL/R_directory/ppt_pics1"

# Auto-create folder if missing
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

ggsave(
  filename = "1_hist_num_stores.png",
  plot = p_hist,
  path = out_folder,   # <-- change folder here
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)



```


```{r}
#------------#
# 1. Metrics #
#------------#

# city_summary has columns: city, num_stores

# Mode helper
get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

n_cities <- nrow(city_summary)

# City with highest stores
idx_max      <- which.max(city_summary$num_stores)
city_max     <- city_summary$city[idx_max]
max_stores   <- city_summary$num_stores[idx_max]

median_stores <- median(city_summary$num_stores, na.rm = TRUE)
mode_stores   <- get_mode(city_summary$num_stores)
mean_stores   <- mean(city_summary$num_stores, na.rm = TRUE)
total_stores  <- sum(city_summary$num_stores, na.rm = TRUE)

metrics_df <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Number of cities",
    "Total stores across all cities",
    "City with highest stores",
    "Number of stores in that city",
    "Median stores per city",
    "Mode of stores per city",
    "Mean stores per city"
  ),
  Value = c(
    n_cities,
    total_stores,
    city_max,
    max_stores,
    round(median_stores, 2),
    mode_stores,
    round(mean_stores, 2)
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#--------------------------#
# 2. Create table as grob  #
#--------------------------#

tbl_grob <- tableGrob(
  metrics_df,
  rows = NULL,                 # no row names
  theme = ttheme_minimal(      # cleaner look
    core = list(fg_params = list(cex = 0.9)),
    colhead = list(fg_params = list(fontface = "bold"))
  )
)

#-------------------------------------------#
# 3. Save table as PNG in the same folder   #
#-------------------------------------------#

# Use the same out_folder you used earlier
# Example:
# out_folder <- "C:/Users/Rahul/Documents/plots/"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "2_city_summary_metrics.png"),
  width = 1600,
  height = 900,
  res = 150
)


grid.draw(tbl_grob)
dev.off()


```


```{r}


state_summary <- store_list %>%
  group_by(state) %>%
  summarise(num_stores = n()) %>%
  ungroup()
bin_width <- 10
min_val   <- min(state_summary$num_stores, na.rm = TRUE)
max_val   <- max(state_summary$num_stores, na.rm = TRUE)

break_min <- floor(min_val / bin_width) * bin_width
break_max <- ceiling(max_val / bin_width) * bin_width

# 2. Histogram with correct bins, correct labels, correct x-axis
p_state_hist <- ggplot(state_summary, aes(x = num_stores)) +
  
  # Histogram bars
  geom_histogram(
    binwidth = bin_width,
    boundary = 0,
    closed   = "right",
    fill     = "steelblue",
    color    = "white"
  ) +
  
  # Labels using new after_stat() syntax
  stat_bin(
    binwidth = bin_width,
    boundary = 0,
    closed   = "right",
    geom     = "text",
    aes(
      y = after_stat(count),
      label = after_stat(count)
    ),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size  = 3
  ) +
  
  # X-axis ticks at bin boundaries
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(break_min, break_max, by = bin_width),
    limits = c(break_min, break_max)
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Stores per State",
    x     = "Number of Stores",
    y     = "Count of States"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

p_state_hist


ggsave(
  filename = "3_hist_stores_per_state.png",
  plot = p_state_hist,
  path = out_folder,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r}
get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

# Metrics
n_states       <- nrow(state_summary)
total_stores   <- sum(state_summary$num_stores, na.rm = TRUE)
median_stores  <- median(state_summary$num_stores, na.rm = TRUE)
mean_stores    <- mean(state_summary$num_stores, na.rm = TRUE)
mode_stores    <- get_mode(state_summary$num_stores)

idx_max        <- which.max(state_summary$num_stores)
state_max      <- state_summary$state[idx_max]
max_stores     <- state_summary$num_stores[idx_max]

# Summary table dataframe
state_metrics_df <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Number of states",
    "Total stores across all states",
    "State with highest stores",
    "Number of stores in that state",
    "Median stores per state",
    "Mean stores per state",
    "Mode stores per state"
  ),
  Value = c(
    n_states,
    total_stores,
    state_max,
    max_stores,
    round(median_stores, 2),
    round(mean_stores, 2),
    mode_stores
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

state_metrics_df
```


```{r}

state_tbl_grob <- tableGrob(
  state_metrics_df,
  rows = NULL,
  theme = ttheme_minimal(
    core    = list(fg_params = list(cex = 0.9)),
    colhead = list(fg_params = list(fontface = "bold"))
  )
)

# Display in RStudio viewer
grid.newpage()
grid.draw(state_tbl_grob)

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "4_state_summary_metrics.png"),
  width  = 1600,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(state_metrics_df)

dev.off()

```

```{r}
type_summary <- store_list %>%
  group_by(type) %>%
  summarise(num_stores = n()) %>%
  ungroup()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Save the type_summary table as PNG
#-------------------------------------------------

# out_folder already exists from previous code
# e.g. out_folder <- "C:/Users/Rahul/Documents/plots/"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "5_type_summary_table.png"),
  width  = 1600,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(type_summary)

dev.off()
```

```{r}
classification_summary <- store_list %>%
  group_by(store_classification) %>%
  summarise(num_stores = n()) %>%
  ungroup()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Save the classification_summary table as PNG
#-------------------------------------------------

# Assumes out_folder already defined earlier
# e.g. out_folder <- "C:/Users/Rahul/Documents/plots/"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "6_classification_summary_table.png"),
  width  = 1600,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(classification_summary)

dev.off()
```

```{r}

area_summary <- store_list %>%
  group_by(store_classification) %>%
  summarise(
    count_stores   = n(),
    min_area_sqft  = min(carpet_area_sq_ft, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_area_sqft = median(carpet_area_sq_ft, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_area_sqft   = round(mean(carpet_area_sq_ft, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    max_area_sqft  = max(carpet_area_sq_ft, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Save the table as PNG
#-------------------------------------------------

# out_folder already defined from earlier code
# e.g. out_folder <- "C:/Users/Rahul/Documents/plots/"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "7_classification_vs_area_table.png"),
  width  = 1800,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(area_summary)

dev.off()

```

```{r}

df_NOB <- read_excel("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_Footfall & NOB.xlsx", 
    sheet = "DATA")
df_NOB <- df_NOB %>%  clean_names()


df_NOB <- merge(df_NOB, store_list[,c("site_code","state","type","carpet_area_sq_ft","store_classification")], by="site_code", all.x=TRUE)



```


```{r}


#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Overall metrics from df_NOB
#-------------------------------------------------

overall_metrics <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Number of stores (unique site_code)",
    "Number of unique weeks",
    "Grand mean NOB",
    "Grand mean Footfall"
  ),
  Value = c(
    n_distinct(df_NOB$site_code),
    n_distinct(df_NOB$week),
    round(mean(df_NOB$nob, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    round(mean(df_NOB$footfall, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Save table as PNG
#-------------------------------------------------

# target folder
out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"

# auto-create
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "8_overall_metrics_table.png"),
  width  = 1600,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(overall_metrics)

dev.off()
```

```{r}

#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Build scatter plot
#-------------------------------------------------

p_scatter <- ggplot(df_NOB, aes(x = footfall, y = nob)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +                       # points with slight transparency
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +        # regression line
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Footfall vs NOB",
    x = "Footfall",
    y = "NOB"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Save as PNG (prefix 9_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

ggsave(
  filename = "9_scatter_nob_vs_footfall.png",
  plot     = p_scatter,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)

```

```{r}

View(df_NOB[is.na(df_NOB$state),])

missing_stuff <- df_NOB[is.na(df_NOB$state),]

city_state_lookup <- df_NOB %>%
  filter(!is.na(state), state != "") %>%
  count(city, state) %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(city, state_inferred = state)

df_NOB_filled <- df_NOB %>%
  left_join(city_state_lookup, by = "city") %>%
  mutate(
    state = dplyr::coalesce(state, state_inferred)
  ) %>%
  select(-state_inferred)


#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Number of malls (unique site_code) per state
#-------------------------------------------------

state_malls <- df_NOB_filled %>%
  filter(!is.na(state), state != "") %>%
  distinct(state, site_code) %>%
  count(state, name = "num_malls")

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Summary metrics table
#-------------------------------------------------

# Helper: mode
get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

n_states      <- nrow(state_malls)
total_malls   <- sum(state_malls$num_malls)
mean_malls    <- round(mean(state_malls$num_malls), 2)
median_malls  <- round(median(state_malls$num_malls), 2)
mode_malls    <- get_mode(state_malls$num_malls)
min_malls     <- min(state_malls$num_malls)
max_malls     <- max(state_malls$num_malls)

idx_max       <- which.max(state_malls$num_malls)
state_max     <- state_malls$state[idx_max]
malls_state_max <- state_malls$num_malls[idx_max]

state_mall_metrics <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Number of states",
    "Total malls (unique site_code)",
    "Mean malls per state",
    "Median malls per state",
    "Mode malls per state",
    "Minimum malls per state",
    "Maximum malls per state",
    "State with highest malls",
    "Malls in highest state"
  ),
  Value = c(
    n_states,
    total_malls,
    mean_malls,
    median_malls,
    mode_malls,
    min_malls,
    max_malls,
    state_max,
    malls_state_max
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#-------------------------------------------------
# 4. Save metrics table as PNG (prefix 10_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "10_state_mall_metrics_table.png"),
  width  = 1600,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(state_mall_metrics)

dev.off()


```


```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Number of malls (unique site_code) per city
#-------------------------------------------------

city_malls <- df_NOB %>%
  distinct(city, site_code) %>%
  count(city, name = "num_malls")

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Summary metrics table
#-------------------------------------------------

# Helper: mode
get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

n_cities        <- nrow(city_malls)
total_malls     <- sum(city_malls$num_malls)
mean_malls      <- round(mean(city_malls$num_malls), 2)
median_malls    <- round(median(city_malls$num_malls), 2)
mode_malls      <- get_mode(city_malls$num_malls)
min_malls       <- min(city_malls$num_malls)
max_malls       <- max(city_malls$num_malls)

idx_max         <- which.max(city_malls$num_malls)
city_max        <- city_malls$city[idx_max]
malls_city_max  <- city_malls$num_malls[idx_max]

city_mall_metrics <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Number of cities",
    "Total malls (unique site_code)",
    "Mean malls per city",
    "Median malls per city",
    "Mode malls per city",
    "Minimum malls per city",
    "Maximum malls per city",
    "City with highest malls",
    "Malls in highest city"
  ),
  Value = c(
    n_cities,
    total_malls,
    mean_malls,
    median_malls,
    mode_malls,
    min_malls,
    max_malls,
    city_max,
    malls_city_max
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save metrics table as PNG (prefix 11_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "11_city_mall_metrics_table.png"),
  width  = 1600,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(city_mall_metrics)

dev.off()
```

```{r}


#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Count stores per type (including NA as a bar)
#-------------------------------------------------

type_counts <- df_NOB %>%
  mutate(
    type_clean = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(type)        ~ "Missing",
      type == "NA"       ~ "Missing",   # if some are literal "NA"
      TRUE               ~ type
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(site_code, type_clean) %>%   # unique stores by type
  count(type_clean, name = "num_stores") %>%
  arrange(desc(num_stores))

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Bar chart with labels on bars
#-------------------------------------------------

p_type_bar <- ggplot(type_counts, aes(x = type_clean, y = num_stores)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = num_stores),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 3
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Stores by Type",
    x = "Store Type",
    y = "Number of Stores"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_type_bar

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save the bar chart as PNG (prefix 12_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

ggsave(
  filename = "12_store_type_distribution.png",
  plot     = p_type_bar,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)
```


```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Count stores per store_classification (incl. missing)
#-------------------------------------------------

classification_counts <- df_NOB %>%
  mutate(
    classification_clean = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(store_classification)          ~ "Missing",
      store_classification == "NA"         ~ "Missing",  # literal "NA" strings
      TRUE                                 ~ store_classification
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(site_code, classification_clean) %>%   # unique stores by classification
  count(classification_clean, name = "num_stores") %>%
  arrange(desc(num_stores))

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Bar chart with labels
#-------------------------------------------------

p_class_bar <- ggplot(classification_counts,
                      aes(x = classification_clean, y = num_stores)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = num_stores),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 3
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Stores by Store Classification",
    x = "Store Classification",
    y = "Number of Stores"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save the bar chart as PNG (prefix 13_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

ggsave(
  filename = "13_store_classification_distribution.png",
  plot     = p_class_bar,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)
```

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Filter rows with valid store classification
#-------------------------------------------------

df_area_class <- df_NOB %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(store_classification),
    store_classification != "",
    store_classification != "NA"
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Boxplot: carpet area by classification
#-------------------------------------------------

p_box_area <- ggplot(df_area_class,
                     aes(x = store_classification,
                         y = carpet_area_sq_ft)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Carpet Area Distribution by Store Classification",
    x = "Store Classification",
    y = "Carpet Area (sq ft)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save PNG (prefix 14_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

ggsave(
  filename = "14_area_by_store_classification_boxplot.png",
  plot     = p_box_area,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)
```



```{r}

#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Compute average footfall per store
#    Drop rows with missing carpet area or footfall
#-------------------------------------------------

df_area_footfall <- df_NOB %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(carpet_area_sq_ft),
    !is.na(footfall)
  ) %>%
  group_by(site_code, carpet_area_sq_ft) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_footfall = mean(footfall, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Scatter plot: carpet area vs avg footfall
#-------------------------------------------------

p_scatter_area <- ggplot(df_area_footfall,
                         aes(x = carpet_area_sq_ft,
                             y = avg_footfall)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Carpet Area vs Average Footfall (per Store)",
    x = "Carpet Area (sq ft)",
    y = "Average Footfall per Store"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save PNG (prefix 15_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

ggsave(
  filename = "15_carpet_area_vs_avg_footfall.png",
  plot     = p_scatter_area,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)
```

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Compute average NOB per store
#    Drop missing carpet area or nob
#-------------------------------------------------

df_area_nob <- df_NOB %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(carpet_area_sq_ft),
    !is.na(nob)
  ) %>%
  group_by(site_code, carpet_area_sq_ft) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_nob = mean(nob, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Scatter plot: carpet area vs avg nob
#-------------------------------------------------

p_scatter_nob <- ggplot(df_area_nob,
                        aes(x = carpet_area_sq_ft,
                            y = avg_nob)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Carpet Area vs Average NOB (per Store)",
    x = "Carpet Area (sq ft)",
    y = "Average NOB per Store"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save PNG (prefix 16_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"

if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

ggsave(
  filename = "16_carpet_area_vs_avg_nob.png",
  plot     = p_scatter_nob,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)


```

```{r}


# Count observations per store
store_obs <- df_NOB %>%
  group_by(site_code) %>%
  summarise(n_obs = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Keep stores with at least, say, 20 weeks of data
# and then take top 16 by number of observations
stores_keep <- store_obs %>%
  filter(n_obs >= 20) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_obs)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 16) %>%          # change 16 -> 9, 12, etc. as you like
  pull(site_code)

df_panel <- df_NOB %>%
  filter(site_code %in% stores_keep,
         !is.na(footfall),
         !is.na(nob))

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Faceted scatter plot: one panel per store
#-------------------------------------------------

p_panel <- ggplot(df_panel, aes(x = footfall, y = nob)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ site_code, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title = "NOB vs Footfall by Store (Selected Stores)",
    x = "Footfall",
    y = "NOB"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6)
  )

p_panel

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "9c_nob_vs_footfall_store_panels.png",
  plot     = p_panel,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 10,
  height   = 7,
  dpi      = 300
)
```

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Demean NOB and Footfall within each store
#-------------------------------------------------

df_demeaned <- df_NOB %>%
  filter(!is.na(nob), !is.na(footfall)) %>%
  group_by(site_code) %>%
  mutate(
    nob_dm      = nob - mean(nob, na.rm = TRUE),
    footfall_dm = footfall - mean(footfall, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Scatter plot of demeaned values
#-------------------------------------------------

p_dm_scatter <- ggplot(df_demeaned,
                       aes(x = footfall_dm, y = nob_dm)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Demeaned Scatter: Footfall vs NOB (Within-Store)",
    x = "Demeaned Footfall",
    y = "Demeaned NOB"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save PNG (prefix 9b_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "9b_demeaned_scatter_footfall_vs_nob.png",
  plot     = p_dm_scatter,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)


```

```{r}
#use only first 4 chars in fy_year
df_NOB <- df_NOB %>%
  mutate(fy_year_short = str_sub(fy_year, 1, 4))

#as.numeric
df_NOB <- df_NOB %>%
  mutate(fy_year_num = as.numeric(fy_year_short))

# replace fy_year with fy_year_num
df_NOB <- df_NOB %>%
  select(-fy_year, -fy_year_short) %>%
  rename(fy_year = fy_year_num)



```

```{r}
df_wave <- df_NOB %>%
  # Drop rows where week or fy_year is missing
  filter(!is.na(fy_year), !is.na(week)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract numeric week index from "Week01", "Week02", ...
    week_num = as.integer(str_remove(week, "^Week")),
    # Make sure fy_year is numeric; if it's a factor/character, convert
    fy_year_num = as.numeric(as.character(fy_year))
  )

# Handle case where conversion fails
if (any(is.na(df_wave$fy_year_num))) {
  stop("fy_year could not be safely converted to numeric. Check its format.")
}

# Get max week and min year to build continuous wave index
max_week   <- max(df_wave$week_num, na.rm = TRUE)
min_year   <- min(df_wave$fy_year_num, na.rm = TRUE)

df_wave <- df_wave %>%
  mutate(
    wave = (fy_year_num - min_year) * max_week + week_num
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Demean footfall and nob within each store
#-------------------------------------------------

df_wave_dm <- df_wave %>%
  filter(!is.na(footfall), !is.na(nob)) %>%
  group_by(site_code) %>%
  mutate(
    footfall_dm = footfall - mean(footfall, na.rm = TRUE),
    nob_dm      = nob      - mean(nob,      na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Aggregate to average demeaned values per wave
#-------------------------------------------------

wave_trends <- df_wave_dm %>%
  group_by(wave) %>%
  summarise(
    footfall_dm_mean = mean(footfall_dm, na.rm = TRUE),
    nob_dm_mean      = mean(nob_dm,      na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# 4. Plot 1: wave vs average demeaned footfall
#-------------------------------------------------

p_wave_footfall <- ggplot(wave_trends,
                          aes(x = wave, y = footfall_dm_mean)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = "loess") +
  labs(
    title = "Time Trend: Demeaned Footfall (Average Across Stores)",
    x = "Wave (Continuous FY Year + Week)",
    y = "Average Demeaned Footfall"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 5. Plot 2: wave vs average demeaned NOB
#-------------------------------------------------

p_wave_nob <- ggplot(wave_trends,
                     aes(x = wave, y = nob_dm_mean)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = "loess") +
  labs(
    title = "Time Trend: Demeaned NOB (Average Across Stores)",
    x = "Wave (Continuous FY Year + Week)",
    y = "Average Demeaned NOB"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 6. Save both plots as PNGs with prefixes 17_ and 18_
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "17_wave_vs_demeaned_footfall.png",
  plot     = p_wave_footfall,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "18_wave_vs_demeaned_nob.png",
  plot     = p_wave_nob,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)
```


```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Build continuous wave variable (fy_year + week)
#-------------------------------------------------

df_wave <- df_NOB %>%
  filter(!is.na(fy_year), !is.na(week)) %>%
  mutate(
    week_num    = as.integer(str_remove(week, "^Week")),
    fy_year_num = as.numeric(as.character(fy_year))
  )

if (any(is.na(df_wave$fy_year_num))) {
  stop("fy_year could not be safely converted to numeric. Check its format.")
}

max_week <- max(df_wave$week_num, na.rm = TRUE)
min_year <- min(df_wave$fy_year_num, na.rm = TRUE)

df_wave <- df_wave %>%
  mutate(
    wave = (fy_year_num - min_year) * max_week + week_num
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Demean NOB and Footfall within each store
#-------------------------------------------------

df_wave_dm <- df_wave %>%
  filter(!is.na(footfall), !is.na(nob)) %>%
  group_by(site_code) %>%
  mutate(
    footfall_dm = footfall - mean(footfall, na.rm = TRUE),
    nob_dm      = nob      - mean(nob,      na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Scatter: wave vs demeaned Footfall (all store-weeks)
#-------------------------------------------------

p_wave_footfall_pts <- ggplot(df_wave_dm,
                              aes(x = wave, y = footfall_dm)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Time Trend: Demeaned Footfall (Store-Level, All Points)",
    x = "Wave (Continuous FY Year + Week)",
    y = "Demeaned Footfall"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 4. Scatter: wave vs demeaned NOB (all store-weeks)
#-------------------------------------------------

p_wave_nob_pts <- ggplot(df_wave_dm,
                         aes(x = wave, y = nob_dm)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Time Trend: Demeaned NOB (Store-Level, All Points)",
    x = "Wave (Continuous FY Year + Week)",
    y = "Demeaned NOB"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 5. Save both plots as PNGs (prefix 19_ and 20_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "19_wave_vs_demeaned_footfall_points.png",
  plot     = p_wave_footfall_pts,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "20_wave_vs_demeaned_nob_points.png",
  plot     = p_wave_nob_pts,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)
```


```{r}
df_sales <- read_excel("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_Sales & Inven.xlsx", 
    sheet = "SalesData")

df_sales <- df_sales %>% clean_names()


colnames(df_sales)
```


```{r}
df_first12 <- df_sales 

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Build summary table
#-------------------------------------------------

col_summary <- tibble(
  column_name = names(df_first12),
  data_type   = sapply(df_first12, class),
  unique_vals = df_first12 %>% map_int(~ n_distinct(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
  
  # up to 10 distinct values
  sample_unique = df_first12 %>%
    map_chr(function(v){
      vals <- unique(v)
      vals <- vals[!is.na(vals)]
      vals10 <- head(vals, 3)
      paste(vals10, collapse = ", ")
    })
)

# If a column has more than 10 uniques, append "... "
col_summary <- col_summary %>%
  mutate(
    sample_unique = ifelse(
      unique_vals > 10,
      paste0(sample_unique, " ..."),
      sample_unique
    )
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save table as PNG (prefix 21_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "21_col_summary.png"),
  width = 2000,
  height = 1200,
  res = 150
)

grid.table(col_summary)

dev.off()
```

```{r}

df_sales_2025 <- df_sales %>%
  filter(as.numeric(year) == 2025)

product_option_counts <- df_sales_2025 %>%
  group_by(new_simplified_product) %>%
  summarise(
    n_options = n_distinct(option_code),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Compute summary metrics
#-------------------------------------------------

# Helper: mode function
get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

mean_opts   <- round(mean(product_option_counts$n_options), 2)
median_opts <- round(median(product_option_counts$n_options), 2)
mode_opts   <- get_mode(product_option_counts$n_options)
min_opts    <- min(product_option_counts$n_options)
max_opts    <- max(product_option_counts$n_options)

product_min <- product_option_counts %>%
  filter(n_options == min_opts) %>%
  slice(1) %>%                     # in case of ties, just take one
  pull(new_simplified_product)

product_max <- product_option_counts %>%
  filter(n_options == max_opts) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(new_simplified_product)

summary_table <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Mean number of option_codes per product",
    "Median number of option_codes per product",
    "Mode number of option_codes per product",
    "Minimum number of option_codes per product",
    "Maximum number of option_codes per product",
    "Product with minimum option_codes",
    "Product with maximum option_codes"
  ),
  Value = c(
    mean_opts,
    median_opts,
    mode_opts,
    min_opts,
    max_opts,
    product_min,
    product_max
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#-------------------------------------------------
# 4. Save summary table as PNG (prefix 22_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "22_product_option_descriptives_2025.png"),
  width  = 2000,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(summary_table)

dev.off()
```

```{r}
top5_products <- product_option_counts %>%
  arrange(desc(n_options)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

#-------------------------------------------------
# 4. Horizontal bar chart (largest on top)
#-------------------------------------------------

p_top5 <- ggplot(
  top5_products,
  aes(x = reorder(new_simplified_product, -n_options),  # note the minus sign
      y = n_options)
) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n_options),
    hjust = -0.2,
    size = 3
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 5 Products by Option Count (2025)",
    x = "Product",
    y = "Number of Option Codes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#-------------------------------------------------
# 5. Save PNG (prefix 23_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

ggsave(
  filename = "23_top5_products_option_count.png",
  plot     = p_top5,
  path     = out_folder,
  width    = 9,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)
```


```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Brand-level totals for NSV and QTY
#-------------------------------------------------

brand_summ <- df_sales_2025 %>%
  group_by(new_brand_description) %>%
  summarise(
    total_nsv = sum(sales_nsv, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_qty = sum(sales_qty, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# Helper: mode function
#-------------------------------------------------

get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

#=================================================
# PART A — NSV Summary Metrics
#=================================================

mean_nsv   <- round(mean(brand_summ$total_nsv), 2)
median_nsv <- round(median(brand_summ$total_nsv), 2)
mode_nsv   <- get_mode(brand_summ$total_nsv)
min_nsv    <- min(brand_summ$total_nsv)
max_nsv    <- max(brand_summ$total_nsv)

brand_low_nsv <- brand_summ %>%
  filter(total_nsv == min_nsv) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(new_brand_description)

brand_high_nsv <- brand_summ %>%
  filter(total_nsv == max_nsv) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(new_brand_description)

summary_nsv <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Mean NSV per brand",
    "Median NSV per brand",
    "Mode NSV per brand",
    "Minimum NSV per brand",
    "Maximum NSV per brand",
    "Brand with lowest NSV",
    "Brand with highest NSV"
  ),
  Value = c(
    mean_nsv,
    median_nsv,
    mode_nsv,
    min_nsv,
    max_nsv,
    brand_low_nsv,
    brand_high_nsv
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Save PNG prefix 24_
out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "24_brand_nsv_summary.png"),
  width  = 2000,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(summary_nsv)

dev.off()


#=================================================
# PART B — QTY Summary Metrics
#=================================================

mean_qty   <- round(mean(brand_summ$total_qty), 2)
median_qty <- round(median(brand_summ$total_qty), 2)
mode_qty   <- get_mode(brand_summ$total_qty)
min_qty    <- min(brand_summ$total_qty)
max_qty    <- max(brand_summ$total_qty)

brand_low_qty <- brand_summ %>%
  filter(total_qty == min_qty) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(new_brand_description)

brand_high_qty <- brand_summ %>%
  filter(total_qty == max_qty) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(new_brand_description)

summary_qty <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Mean QTY per brand",
    "Median QTY per brand",
    "Mode QTY per brand",
    "Minimum QTY per brand",
    "Maximum QTY per brand",
    "Brand with lowest QTY",
    "Brand with highest QTY"
  ),
  Value = c(
    mean_qty,
    median_qty,
    mode_qty,
    min_qty,
    max_qty,
    brand_low_qty,
    brand_high_qty
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Save PNG prefix 25_
png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "25_brand_qty_summary.png"),
  width  = 2000,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(summary_qty)

dev.off()
```

```{r}


#-------------------------------------------------
# 1. Use only 2025 data
#-------------------------------------------------

df_sales_2025 <- df_sales %>%
  filter(as.numeric(year) == 2025)
type_sales_summary <- df_sales_2025 %>%
  group_by(type) %>%
  summarise(
    total_nsv  = sum(sales_nsv, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_qty  = sum(sales_qty, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_nsv))

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save summary df as PNG (prefix 26_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "26_type_vs_sales_summary.png"),
  width  = 1600,
  height = 900,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(type_sales_summary)

dev.off()
```






```{r}
pyramid_sales_summary <- df_sales_2025 %>%
  group_by(fashion_pyramid) %>%
  summarise(
    total_nsv = sum(sales_nsv, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_qty = sum(sales_qty, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_nsv))    # sorted so most valuable pyramid is on top

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Save summary df as PNG (prefix 27_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)

png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "27_fashion_pyramid_vs_sales_summary.png"),
  width  = 1800,
  height = 1100,
  res    = 150
)

gridExtra::grid.table(pyramid_sales_summary)

dev.off()
```

```{r}
ag_sales_summary <- df_sales_2025 %>%
  group_by(ag_description) %>%
  summarise(
    total_nsv = sum(sales_nsv, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_qty = sum(sales_qty, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Create two ordered views:
#    - By NSV
#    - By QTY
#-------------------------------------------------

ag_nsv_summary <- ag_sales_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(total_nsv))

ag_qty_summary <- ag_sales_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(total_qty))

#-------------------------------------------------
# 4. Save both tables as PNGs (prefix 28_ and 29_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) {
  dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

# 28_ — ordered by NSV
png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "28_ag_description_summary.png"),
  width  = 2200,
  height = 1400,
  res    = 150
)
gridExtra::grid.table(ag_nsv_summary)
dev.off()


```

```{r}

#-------------------------------------------------
# 2. Aggregate NSV & QTY per new_simplified_product
#-------------------------------------------------

product_sales <- df_sales_2025 %>%
  group_by(new_simplified_product) %>%
  summarise(
    total_nsv = sum(sales_nsv, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_qty = sum(sales_qty, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# Helper: mode function
#-------------------------------------------------

get_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

#=================================================
# PART A — NSV Descriptives
#=================================================

mean_nsv   <- round(mean(product_sales$total_nsv), 2)
median_nsv <- round(median(product_sales$total_nsv), 2)
mode_nsv   <- get_mode(product_sales$total_nsv)
min_nsv    <- min(product_sales$total_nsv)
max_nsv    <- max(product_sales$total_nsv)

product_low_nsv <- product_sales %>%
  filter(total_nsv == min_nsv) %>%
  slice(1) %>% pull(new_simplified_product)

product_high_nsv <- product_sales %>%
  filter(total_nsv == max_nsv) %>%
  slice(1) %>% pull(new_simplified_product)

summary_nsv <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Mean NSV per product",
    "Median NSV per product",
    "Mode NSV per product",
    "Minimum NSV per product",
    "Maximum NSV per product",
    "Product with lowest NSV",
    "Product with highest NSV"
  ),
  Value = c(
    mean_nsv,
    median_nsv,
    mode_nsv,
    min_nsv,
    max_nsv,
    product_low_nsv,
    product_high_nsv
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#=================================================
# PART B — QTY Descriptives
#=================================================

mean_qty   <- round(mean(product_sales$total_qty), 2)
median_qty <- round(median(product_sales$total_qty), 2)
mode_qty   <- get_mode(product_sales$total_qty)
min_qty    <- min(product_sales$total_qty)
max_qty    <- max(product_sales$total_qty)

product_low_qty <- product_sales %>%
  filter(total_qty == min_qty) %>%
  slice(1) %>% pull(new_simplified_product)

product_high_qty <- product_sales %>%
  filter(total_qty == max_qty) %>%
  slice(1) %>% pull(new_simplified_product)

summary_qty <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Mean QTY per product",
    "Median QTY per product",
    "Mode QTY per product",
    "Minimum QTY per product",
    "Maximum QTY per product",
    "Product with lowest QTY",
    "Product with highest QTY"
  ),
  Value = c(
    mean_qty,
    median_qty,
    mode_qty,
    min_qty,
    max_qty,
    product_low_qty,
    product_high_qty
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)


#-------------------------------------------------
# 3. Export both as PNGs (prefixes 29_ and 30_)
#-------------------------------------------------

out_folder <- "ppt_pics1"
if (!dir.exists(out_folder)) dir.create(out_folder, recursive = TRUE)

# --- NSV table (29_) ---
png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "29_newsimplifiedproduct_nsv_descriptives.png"),
  width  = 2000,
  height = 1000,
  res    = 150
)
gridExtra::grid.table(summary_nsv)
dev.off()

# --- QTY table (30_) ---
png(
  filename = file.path(out_folder, "30_newsimplifiedproduct_qty_descriptives.png"),
  width  = 2000,
  height = 1000,
  res    = 150
)
gridExtra::grid.table(summary_qty)
dev.off()

```

```{r}

# Folder containing your images
img_dir <- "ppt_pics1"

# 1. List all image files (PNG/JPG)
img_files <- list.files(
  path    = img_dir,
  pattern = "\\.(png|jpe?g)$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

if (length(img_files) == 0) {
  stop("No PNG/JPG files found in folder: ", img_dir)
}

# 2. Build data frame for sorting and titles
df_imgs <- data.frame(
  path = img_files,
  file = basename(img_files),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Extract prefix before first underscore (e.g. "9", "9b", "14", etc.)
df_imgs$prefix <- sub("_.*$", "", df_imgs$file)

# Numeric part: leading digits
df_imgs$number <- as.integer(str_extract(df_imgs$prefix, "^[0-9]+"))

# Suffix: whatever is left after leading digits (no lookbehind)
df_imgs$suffix <- sub("^[0-9]+", "", df_imgs$prefix)
df_imgs$suffix[is.na(df_imgs$suffix)] <- ""

# Sort first by number, then by suffix, then by filename
df_imgs <- df_imgs[order(df_imgs$number, df_imgs$suffix, df_imgs$file), ]

# 3. Build slide titles
#    - Drop everything before the first underscore
#    - Drop extension
#    - Replace underscores with spaces
#    - Title-case each word
df_imgs$title_raw <- ifelse(
  grepl("_", df_imgs$file),
  sub("^[^_]*_", "", df_imgs$file),  # keep after first underscore
  df_imgs$file
)

df_imgs$title_raw <- sub("\\.[^.]+$", "", df_imgs$title_raw)  # drop extension
df_imgs$title_raw <- gsub("_", " ", df_imgs$title_raw)        # underscores → spaces

df_imgs$title <- str_to_title(df_imgs$title_raw)

# 4. Create PowerPoint and add one slide per image
ppt <- read_pptx()

# If you want to see available layouts:
# layout_summary(ppt)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(df_imgs))) {
  ppt <- add_slide(ppt, layout = "Title and Content", master = "Office Theme")
  
  # Add title
  ppt <- ph_with(
    ppt,
    value    = df_imgs$title[i],
    location = ph_location_type(type = "title")
  )
  
  # Add picture (body placeholder)
  ppt <- ph_with(
    ppt,
    value    = external_img(df_imgs$path[i]),
    location = ph_location_type(type = "body")
  )
}

# 5. Save the PPT
print(ppt, target = "ppt_pics1_deck.pptx")

```


```{r}
df_inventory <- read_excel("C:/Users/rahul/OneDrive - BITSoM/Desktop/PhD/ABFRL/R_directory/Data/Swayam_Sharma_Sales & Inven.xlsx", 
    sheet = "InvenData")

df_inventory <- df_inventory %>% clean_names()
colnames(df_inventory)

```

```{r}

df_wave <- df_inventory %>%
  # Drop rows where week or fy_year is missing
  filter(!is.na(year), !is.na(week)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract numeric week index from "Week01", "Week02", ...
    week_num = as.integer(str_remove(week, "^Week")),
    # Make sure fy_year is numeric; if it's a factor/character, convert
    fy_year_num = as.numeric(as.character(year))
  )


# Handle case where conversion fails
if (any(is.na(df_wave$fy_year_num))) {
  stop("year could not be safely converted to numeric. Check its format.")
}

# Get max week and min year to build continuous wave index
max_week   <- max(df_wave$week_num, na.rm = TRUE)
min_year   <- min(df_wave$fy_year_num, na.rm = TRUE)
df_wave <- df_wave %>%
  mutate(
    wave = (fy_year_num - min_year) * max_week + week_num
  )




```

```{r}


df_inventory_2025 <- df_inventory %>%
  filter(as.numeric(year) == 2025)
```


```{r}


```
